Common_Genes: OsGSTU (endogenous glutathione S-transferase, transporter gene; activated/upregulated in PER endosperm by the rebuilt MBW complex, facilitating anthocyanin transport; validated by semi-quantitative RT-PCR in Figure 5)
Pathway_Genes: SsCHS (Solenostemon scutellarioides chalcone synthase; transgene driven by endosperm-specific promoter; expressed at high levels in PER endosperm; lines lacking SsCHS showed reduced anthocyanin content; validated by RT-PCR in Figure 5 and PCR in Supplemental Figures 9-10)

SsCHI (Solenostemon scutellarioides chalcone isomerase; transgene driven by endosperm-specific promoter; expressed at high levels in PER endosperm; lines lacking SsCHI showed reduced anthocyanin content; validated by RT-PCR in Figure 5 and PCR in Supplemental Figures 9-10)

SsF3H (Solenostemon scutellarioides flavanone 3-hydroxylase; transgene driven by endosperm-specific promoter; essential because endogenous OsF3H is not expressed in endosperm; validated by RT-PCR in Figure 5)

SsF3'H (Solenostemon scutellarioides flavonoid 3'-hydroxylase; transgene driven by endosperm-specific promoter; expressed at high levels in PER endosperm; lines lacking SsF3'H showed reduced anthocyanin; validated by RT-PCR in Figure 5 and PCR in Supplemental Figures 9-10)

SsDFR (Solenostemon scutellarioides dihydroflavonol 4-reductase; transgene driven by endosperm-specific promoter; essential because endogenous OsDFR is defective; line H#12 lacking SsDFR did not produce purple seeds; validated by RT-PCR in Figure 5)

SsANS (Solenostemon scutellarioides anthocyanidin synthase; transgene driven by endosperm-specific promoter; essential because endogenous OsANS is not expressed in endosperm; validated by RT-PCR in Figure 5)

OsPAL (Oryza sativa phenylalanine ammonia-lyase; endogenous upstream phenylalanine pathway gene; activated/upregulated in PER endosperm by the rebuilt MBW complex; validated by semi-quantitative RT-PCR in Figure 5)

OsC4H (Oryza sativa cinnamate 4-hydroxylase; endogenous upstream phenylalanine pathway gene; activated/upregulated in PER endosperm by the rebuilt MBW complex; validated by semi-quantitative RT-PCR in Figure 5)

Os4CL (Oryza sativa 4-coumarate:CoA ligase; endogenous upstream phenylalanine pathway gene; activated/upregulated in PER endosperm by the rebuilt MBW complex; validated by semi-quantitative RT-PCR in Figure 5)
OsCHS (Oryza sativa chalcone synthase; endogenous structural gene; activated in PER endosperm by the rebuilt MBW complex; validated by semi-quantitative RT-PCR in Figure 5)
OsCHI (Oryza sativa chalcone isomerase; endogenous structural gene; activated in PER endosperm by the rebuilt MBW complex; validated by semi-quantitative RT-PCR in Figure 5)
OsF3'H (Oryza sativa flavonoid 3'-hydroxylase; endogenous structural gene; activated in PER endosperm by the rebuilt MBW complex; validated by semi-quantitative RT-PCR in Figure 5)

OsDFR (Oryza sativa dihydroflavonol 4-reductase; endogenous structural gene; defective in ZH11 and HG1 varieties; upregulated in PER endosperm; validated by sequence analysis in Supplemental Figure 4 and RT-PCR in Figure 5)

Os3GT (Oryza sativa UDP-glucose:flavonoid 3-O-glucosyltransferase; endogenous decorating gene; activated/upregulated in PER endosperm by the rebuilt MBW complex; responsible for glycosylation of anthocyanidins to produce C3G; validated by semi-quantitative RT-PCR in Figure 5)

OsOMT (Oryza sativa O-methyltransferase; endogenous decorating gene; activated/upregulated in PER endosperm by the rebuilt MBW complex; responsible for methylation of C3G to produce P3G; validated by semi-quantitative RT-PCR in Figure 5)
Regulation_Genes: ZmLc (Zea mays Leaf color; bHLH-type transcription factor; transgene driven by endosperm-specific promoter; essential for rebuilding MBW complex to activate anthocyanin biosynthesis genes; two lines Z#8 and H#9 lacking ZmLc produced white grains confirming its requirement; validated by RT-PCR in Figure 5 and PCR in Supplemental Figure 9)

ZmPl (Zea mays Purple leaf; MYB-type transcription factor, homolog of ZmC1 with stronger activity; transgene driven by endosperm-specific promoter; works with ZmLc and endogenous OsWD40 to form MBW complex; validated by RT-PCR in Figure 5)

OsWD40 (Oryza sativa WD40-type transcription factor; endogenous regulatory gene; upregulated in PER endosperm, probably by the rebuilt MBW complex; participates in the MBW transcription factor complex that activates anthocyanin biosynthesis genes; validated by semi-quantitative RT-PCR in Figure 5)
Title: Development of ''Purple Endosperm Rice'' by Engineering Anthocyanin Biosynthesis in the Endosperm with a High-Efficiency Transgene Stacking System
